summary: Fixed AC-1 by replacing the toolbar-height spacing-rung bridge with a dedicated component-toolbar token API (`56.dp`), kept LSToolbar and LSNavHeader on the corrected shared source, and updated the AC-1 unit test to assert canonical 56dp independently of `theme.toolbarComponentSizing.toolbarHeight`.

evidence: RED proof: `cd android && ./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests "com.laneshadow.ui.molecules.LSToolbarTest.default_render_uses_chrome_tokens"` failed with `AssertionError at LSToolbarTest.kt:58` before production fix.
GREEN production fix: `toolbarComponentSizing.toolbarHeight` now resolves from `ToolbarHeightComponentToken = 56.dp` (component token API), not `space.xxxxl`.
Test proof: `default_render_uses_chrome_tokens` now asserts `expectedToolbarHeight = 56.dp` directly, independent of `theme.toolbarComponentSizing.toolbarHeight`.

reviewer_considerations: Focus on `LSToolbar.kt` toolbar-height bridge at `ToolbarHeightComponentToken` and confirm it aligns with `component.toolbarHeight = 56` contract.
Confirm LSNavHeader still consumes corrected value through `theme.toolbarComponentSizing.toolbarHeight` without behavior changes for AC-2..AC-6.
Verify AC-1 test can no longer self-validate via shared helper.
notes: Base SHA: 86f24f890661764f15a7532899954ef4fe3e9d56. Final commit SHA: a184130b921da542dd1bac2308635715c0d5bdf1. RED/GREEN captured as above.
